title: BHAGA Analytics
description: 5-section dashboard: Daily Sales, Weekly Sales, Labor, Order Quality, Payroll. Powered by BQ views from migrations 004 + 005.

list.name: goal_hours_per_net_sales
list.label: Goal: Shift Hrs / Net Sales
list.description: Target shift hours per dollar of net sales (total_hours ÷ net_sales, e.g. 0.02) — rendered as a baseline line on the labor ratio charts

list.name: goal_hours_per_item
list.label: Goal: Shift Hrs / Item
list.description: Target shift hours per item — rendered as a baseline line on the labor ratio charts

targets.rawSql: SELECT TIMESTAMP(date, 'America/Chicago') AS time, orders AS `Orders`, items_sold AS `Items Sold` FROM `jarvis-bhaga-prod.bhaga.vw_model_labor_daily` WHERE date >= DATE('$date_from') ORDER BY date

panels.title: Weekly Order & Item Volume
panels.description: Each bar is one full week (Mon–Sun), labeled by its date range (e.g. '6/1-6/7') so the period a bar covers is explicit. Orders on the left axis, Items Sold on the right. Solid fill.

targets.rawSql: SELECT CONCAT(CAST(EXTRACT(MONTH FROM week_start) AS STRING), '/', CAST(EXTRACT(DAY FROM week_start) AS STRING), '-', CAST(EXTRACT(MONTH FROM DATE_ADD(week_start, INTERVAL 6 DAY)) AS STRING), '/', CAST(EXTRACT(DAY FROM DATE_ADD(week_start, INTERVAL 6 DAY)) AS STRING)) AS `Week`, orders AS `Orders`, items_sold AS `Items Sold` FROM `jarvis-bhaga-prod.bhaga.vw_model_labor_weekly` WHERE week_start >= DATE('$date_from') ORDER BY week_start

SELECT TIMESTAMP(date, 'America/Chicago') AS time, total_hours AS `Total`, hourly_hours AS `Part-time`, fulltime_hours AS `Full-time` FROM `jarvis-bhaga-prod.bhaga.vw_model_labor_daily` WHERE date >= DATE('$date_from') ORDER BY date

panels.title: Daily Hours / Net Sales (total / part-time / full-time)
panels.description: Shift hours per dollar of net sales (hours ÷ net sales), split by total / part-time / full-time. Lower is leaner. Non-positive-sales days are blanked. Dashed line = goal ($goal_hours_per_net_sales).

targets.rawSql: SELECT TIMESTAMP(date, 'America/Chicago') AS time, SAFE_DIVIDE(total_hours, NULLIF(GREATEST(net_sales, 0), 0)) AS `Total`, SAFE_DIVIDE(hourly_hours, NULLIF(GREATEST(net_sales, 0), 0)) AS `Part-time`, SAFE_DIVIDE(fulltime_hours, NULLIF(GREATEST(net_sales, 0), 0)) AS `Full-time`, CAST('$goal_hours_per_net_sales' AS FLOAT64) AS `Goal` FROM `jarvis-bhaga-prod.bhaga.vw_model_labor_daily` WHERE date >= DATE('$date_from') ORDER BY date

panels.title: Daily Hours / Item (total / part-time / full-time)
panels.description: Shift hours per item sold, split by total / part-time / full-time. Lower is leaner. Dashed line = goal ($goal_hours_per_item).

targets.rawSql: SELECT TIMESTAMP(date, 'America/Chicago') AS time, hours_per_item AS `Total`, hourly_hours_per_item AS `Part-time`, fulltime_hours_per_item AS `Full-time`, CAST('$goal_hours_per_item' AS FLOAT64) AS `Goal` FROM `jarvis-bhaga-prod.bhaga.vw_model_labor_daily` WHERE date >= DATE('$date_from') ORDER BY date

panels.title: Weekly Shift Hours
panels.description: Total / part-time / full-time shift hours per week. Each point is plotted at the week-ENDING date (Sunday), so a '6/7' tick = the Mon–Sun week ending 6/7.

targets.rawSql: SELECT TIMESTAMP(week_end, 'America/Chicago') AS time, total_hours AS `Total`, hourly_hours AS `Part-time`, fulltime_hours AS `Full-time` FROM `jarvis-bhaga-prod.bhaga.vw_model_labor_weekly` WHERE week_start >= DATE('$date_from') ORDER BY week_start

panels.title: Weekly Hours / Net Sales (total / part-time / full-time)
panels.description: Shift hours per dollar of net sales (hours ÷ net sales), split by total / part-time / full-time. Lower is leaner. Non-positive-sales weeks are blanked. Dashed line = goal ($goal_hours_per_net_sales). Each point is plotted at the week-ENDING date (Sunday), so a '6/7' tick = the Mon–Sun week ending 6/7.

targets.rawSql: SELECT TIMESTAMP(week_end, 'America/Chicago') AS time, SAFE_DIVIDE(total_hours, NULLIF(GREATEST(net_sales, 0), 0)) AS `Total`, SAFE_DIVIDE(hourly_hours, NULLIF(GREATEST(net_sales, 0), 0)) AS `Part-time`, SAFE_DIVIDE(fulltime_hours, NULLIF(GREATEST(net_sales, 0), 0)) AS `Full-time`, CAST('$goal_hours_per_net_sales' AS FLOAT64) AS `Goal` FROM `jarvis-bhaga-prod.bhaga.vw_model_labor_weekly` WHERE week_start >= DATE('$date_from') ORDER BY week_start

panels.title: Weekly Hours / Item (total / part-time / full-time)
panels.description: Shift hours per item sold, split by total / part-time / full-time. Lower is leaner. Dashed line = goal ($goal_hours_per_item). Each point is plotted at the week-ENDING date (Sunday), so a '6/7' tick = the Mon–Sun week ending 6/7.

targets.rawSql: SELECT TIMESTAMP(week_end, 'America/Chicago') AS time, hours_per_item AS `Total`, hourly_hours_per_item AS `Part-time`, fulltime_hours_per_item AS `Full-time`, CAST('$goal_hours_per_item' AS FLOAT64) AS `Goal` FROM `jarvis-bhaga-prod.bhaga.vw_model_labor_weekly` WHERE week_start >= DATE('$date_from') ORDER BY week_start

panels.title: KDS Time per Item (median / p90 / p95 / p99)
panels.description: Percentiles of per-ITEM prep time per day (order completion time ÷ items in the order). Y-axis capped at 30 min so outliers don't skew the scale.

targets.rawSql: SELECT TIMESTAMP(date, 'America/Chicago') AS time, kds_p90_min AS `p90`, kds_p95_min AS `p95`, kds_p99_min AS `p99`, kds_median_min AS `median` FROM `jarvis-bhaga-prod.bhaga.vw_order_quality_daily` WHERE date >= DATE('$date_from') ORDER BY date

panels.title: Order KDS Times — set your slow threshold with the Min / Item filter
panels.description: One row per order (ticket) with its whole-order KDS prep time (Start → End), and 'On Shift (punched in)' = everyone whose ADP punch overlapped that order's time window. The query does NOT pre-filter — every order in the From-Date window is shown. Set your own slow threshold in-table: click the 'Min / Item' column filter and use the ≥ operator (e.g. ≥ 10) to see only orders over that per-item time; use the 'Date' filter to focus on a single day. 'Min / Item' = Order Min ÷ Items. 'Items in Order' lists every item with its quantity (e.g. '2x Build Your Own BOWL; 1x …'). KDS times are per-order.

targets.rawSql: SELECT date_local, ticket_name, order_source, start_time, end_time, num_items, order_min, ROUND(order_min / num_items, 1) AS min_per_item, (SELECT STRING_AGG(DISTINCT s.employee, ' | ' ORDER BY s.employee) FROM `jarvis-bhaga-prod.bhaga.vw_staff_on_shift` s WHERE s.date = o.date_local AND SAFE.PARSE_TIME('%H:%M', s.in_time) <= SAFE.PARSE_TIME('%H:%M:%S', o.end_time) AND SAFE.PARSE_TIME('%H:%M', s.out_time) >= SAFE.PARSE_TIME('%H:%M:%S', o.start_time)) AS staff_on_shift, items_in_ticket FROM `jarvis-bhaga-prod.bhaga.vw_kds_order_investigation` o WHERE date_local >= '$date_from' ORDER BY min_per_item DESC, date_local DESC, ticket_name

panels.title: Payroll — per Employee per Pay Period
panels.description: Each pay component is shown as a Calculated / ADP / Diff triad. Calculated = our model; ADP = what ADP actually paid; Diff = Calculated − ADP (positive means we computed more than ADP paid). Hourly Pay = hours × wage rate; Tip Pay = allocated tips; Review Bonus = earned review bonus; Total Pay = Hourly + Tip + Bonus. So 'Diff Review Bonus' is our review bonus minus ADP's bonus line, and 'Calculated Total Pay' is the sum of the three calculated components.

targets.rawSql: SELECT CAST(period_start AS STRING) period_start, CAST(period_end AS STRING) period_end, employee, hours_worked, est_gross_pay, adp_wages_paid, wage_diff, tips_allocated, adp_tips_paid, tip_diff, review_bonus, adp_bonus_paid, bonus_diff, est_total_pay, adp_total_paid, ROUND(est_total_pay - adp_total_paid, 2) AS total_diff FROM `jarvis-bhaga-prod.bhaga.vw_model_payroll_period` WHERE period_start >= DATE('$date_from') ORDER BY period_start DESC, employee
